name: Pyrizhky (Baked Turnovers)
recipeIngredient: 1 Large Egg White, Beaten With A
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour and cold butter in a food processor or cut the butter with a pastry cutter into coarse crumbs.
2. Add the egg yolks, salt, and sour cream to the flour mixture. Process or knead lightly until the dough forms a ball.
3. Divide the dough in half and wrap each portion in plastic wrap. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
4. Roll out one half of the dough on a lightly floured work surface to about 1/4-inch thickness.
5. Cut into rounds using a 3-inch cutter or wine glass.
6. Place a spoonful of filling on one side of each round, fold the top over, and seal with a fork or pastry cutter for a decorative edge.
7. Brush each turnover with egg glaze and sprinkle with sugar crystals.
8. Place each turnover on a lightly greased or non-stick baking sheet.
9. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for about 20 minutes. Cool on wire racks.
NOTE:
This is a short dough, so a non-stick baking sheet is best. If unavailable, use as little grease on the sheet as possible with a pastry brush or new paintbrush.
